pi_items.source: verifier.py checks for DASHBOARD_NAME = 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score'
verifier.py requires this exact metric string, and feed_real_data.py maps the real data to this metric name
task.json description directly assigns this role

The actual data mapped to the metric is 'speed_6005.csv' from the NAB realTraffic dataset, which the official NAB README identifies as real highway traffic data from the Twin Cities Metro area (Minnesota Department of Transportation), contradicting the FinTech claim.
export_result.sh dumps the dashboards to this file and verifier.py reads from it

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) realTraffic dataset, specifically highway traffic speed data (speed_6005.csv) collected by the Minnesota Department of Transportation. The metadata claim of 'FinTech / High-Frequency Trading' is contradicted by the actual source of the data. The target dashboard name 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score', the metric 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1', and the result file path are explicitly verified by the verifier script constraints and the data feed configurations.
